name: How Annotation Trains Annotators: Competence Development in Social Influence Recognition
summary: arXiv:2604.02951v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Human data annotation, especially when involving experts, is often treated as an objective reference. However, many annotation tasks are in...
description: arXiv:2604.02951v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Human data annotation, especially when involving experts, is often treated as an objective reference. However, many annotation tasks are inherently subjective, and annotators' judgments may evolve over time. This study investigates changes in the quality of annotators' work from a competence perspective during a process of social influen...
author: Maciej Markiewicz, Beata Bajcar, Wiktoria Mieleszczenko-Kowszewicz, Aleksander Szcz\k{e}sny, Tomasz Adamczyk, Grzegorz Chodak, Karolina Ostrowska, Aleksandra Sawczuk, Jolanta Babiak, Jagoda Szklarczyk, Przemys{\l}aw Kazienko
